## 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

### 5.1 Part A — Short-Answer Questions (3 Marks each)

---

**Q1. [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]**
*Define "joint efficiency" $E$ in the ASME Sec. VIII Div. 1 design of pressure vessels. What values are commonly used?*

**Model Answer (3 marks)**:
Joint efficiency $E$ is a **discount factor** applied to the allowable stress of the parent material to account for the fact that **welded joints are never as strong as the unwelded base metal**. Values per ASME Sec. VIII:

| Examination Level | $E$ |
|---|---|
| **Seamless pipe or plate** | 1.00 |
| **Spot radiography** | 0.70 |
| **100 % radiography** | 0.85 |
| **Full / spot examination as per UG-31** | 0.70 – 0.85 |

It multiplies the allowable stress in the **shell, head, and nozzle reinforcement** equations.

---

**Q2. [KTU University Exam – May 2024]**
*State the three-letter TEMA nomenclature and explain the term "AEL" configuration.*

**Model Answer (3 marks)**:
- The TEMA code is read **Front-End-Shell-Rear-End**.
- **A** = Channel & removable cover (front stationary head)
- **E** = One-pass shell (shell-side flows in a single pass)
- **L** = Fixed tubesheet, bonnet-style rear head (rear header)

This is the most common configuration for clean services on the tube side and easy to maintain.

---

### 5.2 Part B — Long-Answer / Numerical (14 Marks each, Module-2 Internal Choice)

> **KTU Rule**: One of the two choices will be answered by the student. Both choices are given here for reference.

---

#### **Question A (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam – Dec 2022, Model QP]**

A shell-and-tube heat exchanger has the following data:
- Shell ID $D_i = 800$ mm · Design pressure $P = 1.6$ MPa
- Design temperature $T = 180 \, ^\circ\text{C}$ · MOC SA-516 Gr. 70 ($S = 138$ MPa)
- Joint efficiency $E = 0.85$ · Corrosion allowance $c = 2$ mm
- 2:1 ellipsoidal heads, tubes 25 mm OD × 1.6 mm wall · 7 m tubes, triangular pitch 32 mm
- 400 tubes, tube pitch diameter $G = 32$ mm, expanded joint ($\eta = 0.4$)

Compute:
**(a)** Required shell thickness and MAWP. **(7 marks)**
**(b)** Required head thickness and tube-sheet thickness. **(7 marks)**

---

**Model Solution**:

**(a) Shell thickness (7 marks)**

$$
t_s = \frac{P D_i}{2 S E - 1.2 P} + c = \frac{1.6 \times 800}{2 \times 138 \times 0.85 - 1.2 \times 1.6} + 2
$$

$$
= \frac{1280}{234.6 - 1.92} + 2 = \frac{1280}{232.68} + 2 = 5.501 + 2 = 7.501 \text{ mm}
$$

Round up → adopt **10 mm** plate.

MAWP check (with $t = 10$ mm):

$$
\text{MAWP} = \frac{2 S E (t-c)}{D_i + 1.2 (t-c)} = \frac{2 \times 138 \times 0.85 \times 8}{800 + 1.2 \times 8} = \frac{1876.8}{809.6} = 2.318 \text{ MPa}
$$

> $\text{MAWP (2.32 MPa)} \ge P_d \text{ (1.6 MPa)}$ ✅ — **Safe**.

**(b) Head + tube sheet (7 marks)**

Head (2:1 ellipsoidal):

$$
t_h = \frac{P D_i}{2 S E - 0.2 P} + c = \frac{1.6 \times 800}{2 \times 117.3 - 0.2 \times 1.6} + 2
$$

$$
= \frac{1280}{234.28} + 2 = 5.464 + 2 = 7.46 \text{ mm}
$$

Adopt **8 mm** plate.

Tube sheet (TEMA R-method, $F = 1$, $\eta = 0.4$, $G = 32$ mm):

$$
t_{ts} = \frac{F G}{3}\sqrt{\frac{P}{\eta S}} = \frac{1 \times 32}{3}\sqrt{\frac{1.6}{0.4 \times 138}} = 10.667 \times 0.1703 = 1.82 \text{ mm}
$$

> This is the **stress-only** value. **Manufacturing minimum** is **25 mm** for carbon-steel tubesheets and **19 mm** for stainless.
> Adopt $t_{ts} = \mathbf{25 \text{ mm}}$.

**Final BOM extract**:
- Shell plate 10 mm SA-516 Gr. 70
- Head plate 8 mm SA-516 Gr. 70
- Tubesheet 25 mm SA-516 Gr. 70 + 3 mm SS-304 weld overlay
- Tubes 25 mm OD × 1.6 mm SS-304, 400 nos, 7000 mm long

---

#### **Question B (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam – May 2023, Model QP]**

Design the **evaporator vapour body** of a multiple-effect evaporator with the following data:
- Vapour body ID $D_i = 1500$ mm
- Secondary steam pressure = 0.3 MPa (g) inside the body during operation
- Atmospheric pressure outside = 0.1 MPa (a)
- Design temperature $T = 110 \, ^\circ\text{C}$
- MOC SS-304, $S = 115$ MPa, $E = 0.85$ (spot radiograph)
- Corrosion allowance $c = 1$ mm
- Two nozzles required: vapour outlet 400 mm NB and liquor outlet 200 mm NB on opposite sides.

Compute:
**(a)** Design pressure, required shell thickness, MAWP, and a recommendation for stiffening rings. **(7 marks)**
**(b)** Reinforcement for the 400 mm vapour nozzle. **(7 marks)**

---

**Model Solution**:

**(a) Pressure + Shell (7 marks)**

Internal pressure (worst case) = $0.3 + 0.1 = 0.4$ MPa.
Add **10 %** margin (industry practice, though IS doesn't mandate) → $P_d = 0.44$ MPa.

$$
t_s = \frac{P_d D_i}{2 S E - 1.2 P_d} + c = \frac{0.44 \times 1500}{2 \times 115 \times 0.85 - 1.2 \times 0.44} + 1
$$

$$
= \frac{660}{195.5 - 0.528} + 1 = \frac{660}{194.97} + 1 = 3.386 + 1 = 4.386 \text{ mm}
$$

Adopt **6 mm** plate (commercial minimum for 1500 mm SS shell is 5 mm; 6 mm is preferred for rigidity).

MAWP check with $t = 6$ mm:

$$
\text{MAWP} = \frac{2 \times 115 \times 0.85 \times (6-1)}{1500 + 1.2 \times 5} = \frac{977.5}{1506} = 0.649 \text{ MPa}
$$

$\text{MAWP} \ge P_d$ ✅.

**Stiffening rings**: Although the external pressure case is not active here (vapour is on inside, atmospheric on outside → small differential), we should still provide **2 stiffening rings** (one at each nozzle location) for transportation and handling safety, with a 50 mm × 50 mm × 6 mm flat-bar ring.

**(b) Nozzle reinforcement for the 400 mm vapour nozzle (7 marks)**

- Bore $d = 400$ mm
- Nozzle neck $t_n = 6$ mm (same as shell)
- Shell required thickness $t_r = P_d R / (S E) = 0.44 \times 750 / (115 \times 0.85) = 3.38$ mm.
- $F = 1$ (UG-37 single opening).
- Reinforcement height limit = $\min(2.5 t_r, 2.5 t_n, h_{\text{proj}}) = 2.5 \times 3.38 = 8.45$ mm.

$$
A_{\text{req}} = d \, t_r \, F + 2 t_r t_n F_2 = 400 \times 3.38 + 2 \times 3.38 \times 6 = 1352 + 40.56 = 1392.6 \text{ mm}^2
$$

Available area (nozzle excess + pad):

- Nozzle excess thickness $= 6 - 3.38 = 2.62$ mm, over height $2 \times 8.45 = 16.9$ mm.
- $A_1 = 16.9 \times 2.62 = 44.3$ mm²
- Add a 10 mm thick reinforcing pad with effective width $\min(d/2, \text{outer limits})$. Say width $w = 75$ mm on each side.
- $A_2 = 2 \times 75 \times 10 = 1500$ mm²
- Total $A_{\text{avail}} = 44.3 + 1500 = 1544.3$ mm² $\ge A_{\text{req}} = 1392.6$ mm² ✅

**Conclusion**: Provide a 10 mm thick pad, 550 mm OD, welded all around with a 6 mm fillet weld (PWHT not required for 6 mm).

---

### 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Where students lose marks in this topic:**
> 1. **Forgetting corrosion allowance ($c$)** in the numerator *and* in MAWP — examiner deducts **2 marks** typically.
> 2. **Using the wrong formula** for elliptical vs. torispherical vs. hemispherical — remember the denominator coefficients **1.2, 0.2, 0.1**.
> 3. **Rounding $t$ down** to the calculated value instead of rounding **up** to the next commercial plate thickness.
> 4. **Confusing hoop and axial stress** — always use the hoop-stress (×2) case.
> 5. **Tube sheet thickness**: students write 1.8 mm and lose 3 marks; the **manufacturing minimum** (25 mm CS) overrides the formula.
> 6. **Nozzle reinforcement**: not adding the reinforcement from the **gusset / pad** — only the nozzle wall — leading to a deficit.
> 7. **Drawing question**: omitting the **nozzle schedule**, **datum line**, or **centreline** — examiner deducts **1 mark each**.
> 8. **Stiffening rings on vacuum vessels**: forgetting them is a **design error** that fails the vessel.

---

### 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> **Rapid-Revision Checklist (Print This Before the Exam)**

- [ ] **ASME / IS 4503 shell**: $t = PD_i/(2SE - 1.2P) + c$ — remember the **−1.2 P** thick-wall correction.
- [ ] **Ellipsoidal head**: denominator $2SE - 0.2P$; **torispherical** uses $R_c$ and 0.1; **hemispherical** is half of ellipsoidal.
- [ ] **MAWP must exceed $P_d$** — always verify after rounding $t$ up.
- [ ] **Joint efficiency $E$** = 1.0 seamless, 0.85 full radiograph, 0.7 spot radiograph.
- [ ] **Corrosion allowance** is added **once** and subtracted in MAWP.
- [ ] **Tube sheet** TEMA simplified: $t_{ts} = (FG/3) \sqrt{P/(\eta S)}$ — adopt manufacturing minimum 25 mm CS / 19 mm SS.
- [ ] **Nozzle reinforcement UG-37**: total available ≥ required. Pads are usually essential for $>250$ mm nozzles.
- [ ] **Flange bolts**: $W_m = (\pi/4) G^2 P + 2\pi b G m P$, then $n_b = W_m/(A_b S_b)$ rounded up to an even multiple of 4.
- [ ] **Evaporator** body: design for internal pressure (vapour side) and **stiffening rings** if vacuum exists.
- [ ] **Long-tube (Kestner/LTV)** vessels: thin tubes, large $L/D$ → large unsupported length → careful against buckling and vibration.
- [ ] **TEMA three-letter code** = Front · Shell · Rear. AEL = channel-cover / 1-pass shell / fixed tubesheet bonnet.
- [ ] **Drawing pack** = GA + Fabrication + Tube-sheet pattern + Nozzle schedule + BOM.
- [ ] **Souders–Brown** vapour velocity for separator sizing: $u = K \sqrt{(\rho_L - \rho_V)/\rho_V}$.
- [ ] **Downtake area** ≥ total tube cross-section in calandria evaporators.
- [ ] **Stiffening rings** on vacuum vessels every $1.0 - 1.5 \, D$ to prevent buckling.
- [ ] **Safety factor** in code is **4** on ultimate tensile strength (UTS), giving allowable $S = \sigma_u / 4$ at room temperature.
- [ ] **Units used in Indian codes** = MPa (not N/mm² equivalent but stated as MPa) and millimetres for thickness.

> **Final Exam Tip**: If the question says *"Design the shell"*, write all six steps — formula, plug in, calculate, round up, recompute MAWP, state acceptance. Partial credit is awarded per logical step. The **drawing** is worth at least **3 of the 14 marks** — never skip it.
